mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 65% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series grow, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days specifically during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction in late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC consistently near zero) across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation surpasses a 2.5% annual threshold, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 70% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals manufacturing contraction and weakening supply-side momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not materialize in over 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power outside late-cycle regimes.
